title: GitHub Review by sourcery-ai[bot]
summary:
The reviewer wants three things fixed:

1. **DRY up the race-test module list** — the Makefile and CI workflow both independently list which modules to race-test; derive the CI paths from the Makefile (e.g., `make test-race`) so they don't drift apart.
2. **Fix the `OPERATOR` parameter doc** — the Makefile comment says `make test-race [OPERATOR=keystone]` but the target ignores that variable; either wire it up or remove the misleading usage hint.
3. **Align the CI condition in docs** — the plan/description uses shorthand `if: go == 'true'` but the actual YAML uses `if: needs.changes.outputs.go == 'true'`; make them match.
external_feedback:
Hey - I've found 1 issue, and left some high level feedback:

- The list of modules under race testing is duplicated between the Makefile (`internal/common`, `$(OPERATORS)`) and the `test-race` CI job (explicit paths), which risks drift over time; consider deriving the CI paths from the same source (e.g., calling `make test-race` or using `OPERATORS`) so they stay in sync.
- In the CI workflow, the `test-race` job description and DAG snippet use a shorthand `if: go == 'true'`, while the actual job condition is `if: needs.changes.outputs.go == 'true'`; aligning the documented condition with the exact expression used in YAML would avoid confusion for future maintainers.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- The list of modules under race testing is duplicated between the Makefile (`internal/common`, `$(OPERATORS)`) and the `test-race` CI job (explicit paths), which risks drift over time; consider deriving the CI paths from the same source (e.g., calling `make test-race` or using `OPERATORS`) so they stay in sync.
- In the CI workflow, the `test-race` job description and DAG snippet use a shorthand `if: go == 'true'`, while the actual job condition is `if: needs.changes.outputs.go == 'true'`; aligning the documented condition with the exact expression used in YAML would avoid confusion for future maintainers.

## Individual Comments

### Comment 1
<location path="Makefile" line_range="87" />
<code_context>
+# Operator code is inherently concurrent — reconcilers, watches, and informer
+# caches operate across goroutines — so race detection catches data corruption
+# that normal tests miss. Does not include -count=1 (CI adds that separately).
+# Usage: make test-race [OPERATOR=keystone]
+test-race:
+	@echo "Race-testing internal/common module..."
</code_context>
<issue_to_address>
**issue:** The documented `OPERATOR` parameter for `test-race` isn’t actually used by the target.

The usage text suggests `make test-race [OPERATOR=keystone]`, but the target actually iterates over `$(OPERATORS)` and ignores `OPERATOR` passed on the command line. This inconsistency can mislead users who expect to run race tests for a single operator. Please either plumb `OPERATOR` into the target (e.g., only loop over the requested operator) or update the usage comment to match the current behavior.
</issue_to_address>
~~~

</details>

***

code_comments.body:
**issue:** The documented `OPERATOR` parameter for `test-race` isn’t actually used by the target.

The usage text suggests `make test-race [OPERATOR=keystone]`, but the target actually iterates over `$(OPERATORS)` and ignores `OPERATOR` passed on the command line. This inconsistency can mislead users who expect to run race tests for a single operator. Please either plumb `OPERATOR` into the target (e.g., only loop over the requested operator) or update the usage comment to match the current behavior.
code_comments.author: sourcery-ai[bot]
code_comments.resolution: The Makefile comment was updated to clarify that OPERATOR is plumbed through via the existing global override mechanism at lines 13-16 (`ifdef OPERATOR / OPERATORS := $(OPERATOR)`), which already causes the for-loop to iterate over only the requested operator. The usage line was updated to `make test-race [OPERATOR=keystone] [RACE_FLAGS="-count=1"]` to document both parameters accurately, and the comment now explains the CI-specific RACE_FLAGS flow.

resolution_summary: All three review issues were addressed in staged (not yet committed) changes across three files. The CI test-race job was changed from explicit go test commands to `make test-race RACE_FLAGS="-count=1"`, eliminating the module list duplication by delegating to the Makefile as the single source of truth. A new `RACE_FLAGS` variable was added to the Makefile to support CI passing `-count=1`. The DAG shorthand fix from the reviewer's comment about test-race was also applied to three additional conditional jobs in docs/reference/ci-workflow.md (helm-validate, docs, e2e-infra) that had the same shorthand pattern, aligning all four entries with the exact YAML expressions.
